intent: Denials from THREE distinct confined domains (httpd_t, logrotate_t, sshd_t) interleaved in one log, with httpd_t hitting two triples (file + dir). Fixtures the grouped triage REPORT output: per-domain sections, stable ordering, correct grouping of multiple triples under one domain. This is the report-shape oracle (audit2allow -N emits #===== per-domain section headers).
_kinds_note: All TeAllowable. audit2why reports the httpd_t default_t:dir search as 'would be allowed by active policy' (httpd searches default dirs in shipped policy) but it is a valid input record. floor_label none for all.

oracle.audit2allow_N: Per-domain sections: #= httpd_t = { allow httpd_t default_t:dir search; allow httpd_t default_t:file { getattr read }; } #= logrotate_t = { allow logrotate_t var_t:file read; } #= sshd_t = { allow sshd_t default_t:file { open read }; }. The two httpd_t triples (file + dir) are grouped under one domain section.
oracle.audit2allow_R: Per-domain: httpd_t -> files_read_default_files(httpd_t) + files_search_default(httpd_t); logrotate_t -> files_read_var_files(logrotate_t); sshd_t -> files_read_default_files(sshd_t). Every raw denial replaced by a broad interface macro.
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: -R replaces all four raw allows with broad interfaces (files_read_default_files grants read_file_perms on ALL default_t files; files_search_default; files_read_var_files). Our expected.te keeps four exact raw allows grouped per (domain,type,class). The report ORDERING and per-domain grouping is the fixture target.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: The denials are interleaved (httpd, logrotate, sshd, then httpd again - the second httpd denial is OUT of source-order with the first). A report that groups by first-seen order without re-collecting later denials for an already-seen domain would either (a) put the httpd dir:search in a second stray httpd section, or (b) drop it. Correct triage groups ALL httpd_t triples under one httpd_t section regardless of log position, with deterministic ordering. Also tests multi-domain section separation and that each domain's allows stay narrow.
